body.u: I was just eighteen when I finally left school and to me that was like being released from prison for although I like school I just didn’t like all the work. Now I have only two months before I get my ‘A’ level results but I am pretty confident in two of them at least.

I remember those two months went very quickly and I also remberremember my great happiness and sense of achievement when I discovered I had pasted all three ‘A’ levels. To celebrate I remberremember I got drunk but I seem to only recall the hangover in the morning.

Then with a certain pride I went for my R.A.F. interview and exam and got in with little difficult even though it was harder than I expected. I soon found that the R.A.F. to be what I expected and I led an enjoyable and fulfilled time training as an Electronic enginneerengineer and also study for another A level, this time in chemestrychemistry.

I had about six months left in the R.A.F. when I met Julie and I thought I had found the girl of my Dreams but I was to be proved wrong later on. With the final exam over and my new found skill I went for an interview with Masonic Electronics LTD one of the biggest electronic companies around at that time. At the interview I was very nervous but who isn’t but I got over this once the interview was underway. But it seemed the interviewer had a prejudice against R.A.F. trainees and he seemed to give me the hardest questions he could find and I found myself fighting a losing battle. As I realised I didn’t get the job but I wasn’t worried another would turn up. It did and this time I got the job.

My new employers were Grundig and I found the job satisfying and the pay was good. I had been knocking around with Julie and I thought I loved her so I popped the question we were married in June 1988 and I was 25. At first all things went well but gradually we began to drift apart until we were arguing every other minute and the littlest thing could set us of.
It was inevitable that we would get divorced and sued her on the grounds of Adultaryadultery with 6 who I thought to be hilarious at the time. Of all the people she had an affair with the milkman.

Mind you I was {angel?} at the age of thirty-six I had become one of the {randiest?!} managers going. I was on my sixth secartarysecretary when I found it to be more than just elongated one night stand and she became my second wife. We had our honeymoon in Greece and a couple of months later, well actually nine, we had our first child, a girl and what a beauty she looked. Two years later we had twins and because my wife was getting on we decided to have no more. My eldest grew up into a lovely beauty and now at the age of nineteen she has a figure I would have whistled when I was younger. My two boys grew up into fine lads but for some reason I never got in with the younger of the two and he left home very home early and has a criminal record and at the moment he is in a detention centre serving time. The elder one Marc is a fine lad and has been offered a place at the Kent under 21’s cricket team and also he has been {offered} a place at Tottenham but I do not which he shall take if any.
Me I have had a full life and and as I sit here and write this I feel contented and fullfilledfulfilled.
